title: Silky Croatian Rice Bowl
description: A buttery, silky and romantic Croatian dish that blends creamy brown rice noodles with toasted whole grain bread, all while staying kosher-friendly.
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the brown rice noodles for 8-10 minutes until al dente; drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat; add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the butter to the skillet and let it melt, then pour in the almond milk and coconut milk; stir until fully blended and heated to 90-95°C.
Season the sauce with smoked paprika, sea salt, and lemon zest; simmer for 3-4 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Fold in the cooked noodles, ensuring they are evenly coated with the silky sauce; remove from heat.
Toast the whole grain bread slices in a dry pan or toaster until golden; spread a thin layer of butter on each slice while warm.
Arrange the toasted bread on a serving plate; spoon the creamy noodle mixture over the toast, creating a layered effect.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley for a burst of color and freshness.
Serve immediately while warm for the best silky texture.
